task:
Three rounds of user-driven dashboard improvements (binayah-ultimate). 1 round = 1 stage. PM should plan exactly 3 stages.

ROUND 1 — Card polish + safety net:
1. Pencil = full edit mode on card. When editMode=true on a card (Stage in pipeline list, TaskCard in kanban), the WHOLE card becomes editable: rename input, description textarea, priority cycle, archive button — all simultaneously visible/live. Click outside or press Escape commits all edits and exits. No more "click pencil → edit one thing → click pencil again". Add the same per-card editMode pattern to PIPELINE-LEVEL editing (pipeline header in pipeline-list view): pencil button on pipeline header opens pipeline edit mode showing rename + description + priority + archive — gated to captain/firstMate role only.
2. Undo system. New `useUndoStack` hook holds last 5 destructive ops (archiveStage, archivePipeline, archiveSubtask, future deletes). Each op pushes an inverse onto the stack. Surfaces:
   - Toast appearing for 8s after destructive op with "undo" button
   - Persistent ↶ button in Dashboard header (Word-style) — click triggers most recent undo, hover tooltip shows "undo: archived 'test stage'"
   - Cmd/Ctrl+Z keyboard shortcut hooks the same stack
   - Empty stack: header button is grayed/disabled, no toast
3. "Since you were here" divider in stage comments. Track `lastSeenComments[stageId]: ms-timestamp` in localStorage (per-user per-stage). When opening a stage, scan comment IDs (which are timestamps), render a thin `// since you were here` divider line in the comment list above the first comment newer than lastSeen. On stage close (or popover close), update lastSeen[stageId] = Date.now().
4. Activity timeline tab inside expanded stage. Add a tab next to existing Comments tab in the expanded stage view. Renders existing activityLog filtered to entries where target === stage.name OR (subtask key parsing) parentStageId === stage.name. Show entries newest-first with: actor avatar, type icon (claim/status/comment/etc), one-line description, relative time. No new state — pure read view.

ROUND 2 — Trust + conversation:
1. Permissions: Captain/firstMate-only on destructive ops. SERVER ENFORCEMENT: PATCH /api/pipeline-state route checks session user against the workspace.captains and workspace.firstMates lists for any patch that touches archivedStages, archivedPipelines, archivedSubtasks, customPipelines (delete operations), or workspaces (member changes). Return 403 with {error: "FORBIDDEN", reason: "captain or first-mate role required"} if check fails. Crew can still claim/comment/react/assign/drag own stages — those stay open. UI gating: useRole(workspaceId) hook in ModelContext returns "captain"|"firstMate"|"crew"|null. Pencil button only renders for users with editing rights on that pipeline. For destructive pipeline ops (archive pipeline, delete pipeline) show "are you sure?" modal naming the consequences ("this pipeline has 14 stages, 3 unclaimed").
2. Anti-jump comment polling. When commentOpen === stageId AND commentInput[stageId] is non-empty (user is typing), incoming polled comments for that stage do NOT merge into comments[stageId] directly. Instead they go into pendingNewComments[stageId] slice. The comment popover shows a "↓ N new" pill at the bottom; clicking it merges and scrolls. If the user is NOT typing (input empty) or popover is closed, comments merge directly (current behavior).
3. @mentions in stage comments. Extend existing parseMentions/notifyMentions logic from src/app/api/pipeline-state/messages/route.ts. Wire it into POST /api/pipeline-state/comments — after the comment is saved, parse text for @username, look up by user.name (case-insensitive, first-name match like chat does), call sendNotifications for each mentioned user. Add @anna autocomplete dropdown in CommentPopover input: when user types @ followed by letters, show a popup of matching users (filter by name prefix), arrow-up/down to select, Tab/Enter to insert "@FirstName ".
4. Comment-level reactions. New state slice commentReactions: Record<commentId(number), Record<emoji, userId[]>>. Atomic POST endpoint /api/pipeline-state/comment-reactions {commentId, emoji} — toggle current user's reaction. Render reaction strip below each comment in the popover (similar visual to stage reaction pills, smaller). Click emoji = toggle. Click + opens emoji picker. ModelContext: add commentReactions, handleCommentReact handler. apiSync.ts: add pushCommentReaction.

ROUND 3 — Reach + momentum:
1. Drag subtask to a different parent stage. Drop target = a stage card itself (not a kanban column). When user drags a subtask card and drops it onto a stage card (not its current parent): copy the subtask under newParent::sameId key, transfer reactions/comments/commentReactions/assignments/subtaskStages entries from old key to new key (updating the new composite key everywhere), then delete old key. Visual feedback: when dragging a subtask, stage cards get a dashed accent border on hover; column drop targets become dimmer. Use the existing SubtaskKey.make/parse helpers — never raw :: splits.
2. Streaks. Server-derived streakByUser computed from activityLog: for each user, count consecutive UTC-day buckets (working back from today's UTC) where the user appears as actor on any claim/comment/status_change activity entry. Emit streakByUser: Record<userId, number> from GET /api/pipeline-state alongside existing state. Client renders the streak in the team bar next to points: "Anna 142pts 🔥7" for a 7-day streak. Render flame emoji + number only when streak >= 2 (don't celebrate single-day "streaks"). No client write path — pure derived state.
3. "While you were away" digest. On Dashboard mount, check localStorage lastSession (ms). If absent OR (Date.now() - lastSession) > 12h, show a centered modal: "while you were away..." with three sections — (a) stages claimed by teammates since lastSession (top 5), (b) comments mentioning you since lastSession (top 5), (c) status changes on stages you claimed since lastSession (top 5). Each entry: actor avatar, one-line description, relative time. Single "got it" button dismisses. After dismiss, set lastSession = Date.now(). Always update lastSession on Dashboard unmount.
4. Mobile single-column "today" view. At viewport width <= 640px, REPLACE the multi-column kanban with a vertical single-column list. Filter: show only stages where current user is in claimers OR is the assignee. Sort order: status priority (blocked > in-progress > planned > active > done > concept). Each row: compact card with title, status pill, claim/done state, ONE primary action button (claim if unclaimed, done if in-progress and mine, view if other status). Touch interactions: swipe-right gesture on a row = toggle claim/unclaim, swipe-left = open comment popover. Use react-swipeable or hammer.js if a lib helps; otherwise pointer events. Drop kanban columns entirely on mobile.

OUT OF SCOPE for these 3 rounds:
- Workspace.pipelineIds → pipeline.workspaceId migration (Stage 3b architecture batch already shipped)
- Schema migrations (we're staying on v3)
- Notification UI redesign (mentions reuse existing notification path)
- Animation overhaul (motion is already standardized to 150/300/1400 from Stage 2)

stages.spec: ## Round 1 — Card polish + safety net ### Why this will be good This stage is the difference between a tool that *works* and one that *forgives you*. Today the dashboard punishes mistakes (no undo) and clicks (pencil → one field → pencil again). After Round 1: every destructive action is reversible from three different surfaces (toast, header button, Cmd+Z), and the pencil becomes a real edit mode that respects the user's flow. The 'since you were here' divider and per-stage activity tab make the dashboard feel alive between sessions — you come back and the product *tells you what changed*. Production-ready means: the undo stack survives across UI surfaces, the divider does not flicker, the timeline tab handles empty state gracefully, and editMode does not break drag-and-drop or claim flows. Benchmark: Linear's Cmd+Z stack, Notion's @ mention drawer (next round), GitHub's 'new since you were here' diff marker. ### The 4 deliverables #### 1. Pencil = full card edit mode (Stage + TaskCard + Pipeline header) **Intent:** A pencil click flips the entire card (or pipeline header) into a single, cohesive edit surface where every editable field is live simultaneously. Not a modal, not a sequence of toggles — the card itself becomes editable in place. Click outside or press Escape commits all changes and exits. **Files to touch:** - `src/components/Stage.tsx` — receive `editMode: boolean` and `onEnterEdit/onExitEdit` from parent. When `editMode` is true, render rename input (replacing the title), description textarea, priority cycle button (clickable with current value visible), archive button. When false, render the existing read view. Outside-click / Escape handler at the card root commits + calls `onExitEdit`. - `src/components/KanbanView.tsx` (TaskCard) — same pattern as Stage. Card-level editMode state lifted to parent or held in `useEphemeral()` keyed by stage id. - `src/components/Dashboard.tsx` — owns `editingCardId: string | null` (a single card edits at a time). Pencil click in any list/kanban sets it; outside-click clears it. Use `useEphemeral()` per existing pattern. - New: pipeline-header pencil. In pipeline-list view (where pipeline headers render), add a pencil button that flips the *header* (not the children) into edit mode: rename input, description textarea, priority cycle, archive button. Same outside-click / Escape commit pattern. **Gated to captain/firstMate role only** — if `useRole(workspace.id)` returns `'crew'` or `null`, pencil does not render. (The role hook lands in Round 2; for Round 1, gate on a placeholder `canEditPipeline()` that currently checks `workspace.captains.includes(currentUser.id) || workspace.firstMates.includes(currentUser.id)` — Round 2 will refactor to the hook.) **Critical constraints:** - TypeScript clean; do not change Stage's existing prop names that ModelContext consumes — only ADD `editMode`, `onEnterEdit`, `onExitEdit`. - editMode must NOT break drag-and-drop. While editMode is true, the card must not be draggable (set `draggable={!editMode}` on the wrapper). Make this explicit, not implicit — DnD silently failing is a bug class to avoid. - editMode must NOT break claim/react/comment buttons — those remain interactive even in edit mode (so a captain can claim while editing). - Outside-click handler must use `pointerdown` not `click` to avoid race with input blur. Standard pattern: ref + global listener + `if (ref.current && !ref.current.contains(e.target)) onExitEdit()`. - Commit semantics: every field is saved as-you-type via existing handlers (rename → handleRename, etc.). 'Commit on exit' just means we exit; nothing extra needed unless DEV finds a perf issue with mid-typing patches (in which case a 300ms debounce in the rename handler is acceptable). - Visual: when editMode is on, the card gets a subtle accent ring (`box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px ${t.accent}33`) so the user can *see* they are editing. Use theme tokens. Reference DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md — 'Every surface responds'. **Acceptance criteria (QA):** - Click pencil on a Stage card in pipeline list: rename input, description textarea, priority button, archive button all visible and live at once. - Type in rename, click priority — both are reflected without leaving edit mode. - Press Escape → all edits committed, card returns to read view. - Click outside the card → same. - Pipeline header pencil: visible only when current user is captain or firstMate of that workspace. - Crew user does NOT see pencil on pipeline header. - editMode card cannot be dragged (visual feedback: cursor stays default, not 'grab'). #### 2. Undo system — `useUndoStack` hook + 3 surfaces **Intent:** Every destructive op (archive stage, archive pipeline, archive subtask) pushes an inverse onto a stack of depth 5. The user can undo via toast (8s window), header button (Word-style ↶), or Cmd/Ctrl+Z. Empty stack → header button is grayed and disabled, no toast. **Files to touch:** - New: `src/lib/hooks/useUndoStack.ts` — hook with shape `{ push: (op: UndoOp) => void, undo: () => void, peek: () => UndoOp | null, size: number }`. Internal state: `UndoOp[]` capped at 5 (FIFO drop oldest on push beyond cap). `UndoOp = { id: string, label: string, inverse: () => void | Promise, createdAt: number }`. Hook is called once at Dashboard root and the resulting object is exposed via context (existing ModelContext or a new lightweight UndoContext — DEV decides which is cleaner; ModelContext is acceptable since undo ops dispatch through model handlers anyway). - `src/components/Dashboard.tsx` — instantiate `useUndoStack()`, expose `pushUndo` to children via context. Add the persistent ↶ button in the Dashboard header next to existing controls. Tooltip on hover shows `peek().label` (e.g. `undo: archived 'test stage'`); disabled state when `size === 0`. Cmd/Ctrl+Z global keydown listener on Dashboard root that calls `undo()` (preventDefault, but only when no input/textarea has focus — standard editor pattern). - `src/components/ui/Toast.tsx` (or wherever toasts live) — add an `undo` variant: `showToast({ text: "archived 'test stage'", undoLabel: 'undo', onUndo: () => ... }, durationMs: 8000)`. Visual: standard toast surface + a right-aligned 'undo' link styled with `t.accent`. Click → calls `onUndo` and dismisses immediately. - Wire push points (the only callers in Round 1): - `archiveStage(stageId)` handler in ModelContext → push `{ label: 'archived ', inverse: () => unarchiveStage(stageId) }` then trigger toast. - `archivePipeline(pipelineId)` → push `{ label: 'archived pipeline ', inverse: () => unarchivePipeline(pipelineId) }` then toast. - `archiveSubtask(parentKey, subtaskId)` → push `{ label: 'archived subtask ', inverse: () => unarchiveSubtask(parentKey, subtaskId) }` then toast. **Critical constraints:** - The inverse functions must be idempotent and safe to call even if the user has navigated away or other state has changed. Implementation: inverse calls existing un-archive handler which is already idempotent (writes to archivedStages map). - Cmd/Ctrl+Z handler MUST detect input focus and not fire when typing in inputs/textareas — otherwise it will fight native text-undo. Use `document.activeElement instanceof HTMLInputElement || HTMLTextAreaElement || isContentEditable`. - Stack is in-memory (not persisted). Page refresh clears it. That is acceptable and intended — undo is for the current session. - Toast `undo` button must be the SAME stack entry that the header button would pop, so user clicking 'undo' in a toast and then immediately Cmd+Z does not re-undo the same op. After invoke, the op is removed from the stack. **Acceptance criteria (QA):** - Archive a stage → toast appears with 'undo' button. Click → stage returns. Toast dismisses. - Archive a stage → wait 8s → toast disappears. Press Cmd+Z → stage still returns (header button not yet exhausted). - Click ↶ header button with empty stack → nothing happens, button visibly disabled (gray). - Hover ↶ when stack has items → tooltip shows last op label. - Archive 6 things → only the last 5 are undoable (oldest dropped). - Type in a comment input and press Cmd+Z → native browser text-undo fires, NOT undo stack. #### 3. "Since you were here" divider in stage comments **Intent:** When a user opens a stage they have visited before, render a thin marker line above the first comment newer than the user's last visit. It is *the* most useful affordance for catching up on async conversation. Update lastSeen on close. **Files to touch:** - localStorage key: `binayah_lastSeenComments_<fixedUserId>` storing `Record<stageId, msTimestamp>`. Helper read/write in `src/lib/storage.ts` (extend existing `lsGet`/`lsSet` patterns; do NOT bump SCHEMA_VERSION — this is additive, missing key = no divider). - `src/components/Stage.tsx` (or wherever the comment popover renders — DEV identifies). On comment list render, find the first comment whose `id > lastSeen[stageId]`. Insert a row above it: `<div>// since you were here</div>` styled with `t.textDim`, Geist Mono, italic, with a thin `t.border` line on either side. If no such comment exists (all are older or all are newer-than-current-session-only), render nothing. - On comment popover close (or stage collapse, whichever ends the viewing session): set `lastSeen[stageId] = Date.now()`. Use a useEffect with cleanup or an explicit onClose handler. **Critical constraints:** - Comment IDs are millisecond timestamps (per task description). Trust this — do not introduce a new ID scheme. - Divider must NOT re-flicker on incoming polled comments during the same viewing session. Solution: capture `lastSeenSnapshot` on mount of the popover and use that snapshot for comparison until the popover closes. On close, write the new (current Date.now()) to localStorage. - If the user has never seen this stage before, the entire comment list is 'new' → do NOT render the divider above the first comment (would be visual noise). Only render if there is at least one comment older than lastSeen AND at least one newer. - Per-user storage — key includes `fixedUserId` so multiple users on shared devices don't pollute each other. **Acceptance criteria (QA):** - Open a stage with 3 old comments. Close it. Have a teammate post a comment. Reopen — divider appears between comment 3 and the new comment. - First-ever visit to a stage with comments → no divider. - Stage with only NEW comments (since lastSeen) → no divider (everything is new, divider is meaningless). - Polled comment arrives mid-viewing-session → it appears below the divider, not above; divider does not jump. #### 4. Activity timeline tab inside expanded stage **Intent:** The expanded stage view today has a Comments tab. Add a sibling Activity tab that filters the existing `activityLog` to entries relevant to this stage and renders newest-first. Pure read view. This is the single-stage version of the global activity feed. **Files to touch:** - `src/components/Stage.tsx` (expanded section) — add tab switcher: `Comments | Activity`. Default tab: Comments (preserves current behavior). Tab state held locally in the Stage component (not persisted). - Activity tab body: reads `activityLog` from ModelContext, filters to `entry.target === stage.name` OR (`entry.target` parses as a SubtaskKey via `SubtaskKey.parse(entry.target)` and the parent stage id matches `stage.name`). Sort newest-first by `entry.time`. Render each entry with: `<AvatarC user={users.find(u => u.id === entry.user)} size={20} t={t} />`, type icon (claim → ✓, status_change → ↗, comment → 💬, react → 🫡, etc — DEV picks consistent set), one-line description (`<actor> <verb> <detail>`), relative time on the right. - Empty state: `// no activity yet` in `t.textDim`, Geist Mono — matches the product's voice. **Critical constraints:** - Pure read view — no new state, no new API. activityLog is already in ModelContext. - Use `SubtaskKey.parse` to check parent linkage, never raw `::` split. Per project guidance. - Reuse the same type→icon mapping that NotificationBell or ActivityFeed already use — do not invent a new visual language for activity types here. If the existing mapping is in `ActivityFeed.tsx`, extract a shared helper to `src/lib/activityIcons.ts` (small refactor, but pays off for consistency). - Tab switcher visual must match DESIGN_SYSTEM.md component patterns — use `t.accent` for active tab underline, `t.textMuted` for inactive label. - Do NOT lazy-load this tab — it's tiny and already rendered inside an expanded stage; the data is in memory. **Acceptance criteria (QA):** - Expand a stage → see two tabs: Comments and Activity. - Click Activity → list of recent activity for THIS stage only, newest first. - Empty activity → '// no activity yet' message. - Each row: avatar + icon + description + relative time. - Subtask comments under this stage appear here (parent-stage filter via SubtaskKey). ### Stage 1 dependencies / handoff to Stage 2 - The placeholder `canEditPipeline()` check used in deliverable 1 is REPLACED in Stage 2 with the real `useRole(workspaceId)` hook. DEV in Stage 1 should write `canEditPipeline()` as a small inline helper that Stage 2 can swap. - The `editMode` plumbing in Stage 1 must already accept being conditionally rendered (Stage 2 hides the pencil entirely for crew). Don't bake 'pencil always renders' into Stage.tsx. - The toast undo surface in Stage 1 will also be consumed by other Round 2/3 ops — DEV should keep `showToast({ undoLabel, onUndo })` generic enough that any future destructive op can plug in. ### Files DEV will touch (summary) - `src/components/Stage.tsx` (editMode props, divider, activity tab) - `src/components/KanbanView.tsx` (editMode on TaskCard) - `src/components/Dashboard.tsx` (editingCardId, undo header button, Cmd+Z listener, pipeline-header pencil rendering) - `src/lib/hooks/useUndoStack.ts` (NEW) - `src/lib/contexts/ModelContext.tsx` (push points for archive ops; expose pushUndo) - `src/components/ui/Toast.tsx` (undo variant) - `src/lib/storage.ts` (lastSeenComments helpers — additive, no SCHEMA_VERSION bump) - `src/lib/activityIcons.ts` (NEW — extracted shared icon map) ### Apply DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md - 'Every surface responds' — editMode must visibly distinguish itself (accent ring). - 'Empty states are part of the game' — undo button disabled state must look intentional, not broken; activity tab empty state in product voice. - 'Motion has meaning' — toast undo, slide-in 200ms; no new motion overhauls. - 'The copy is the culture' — `// since you were here`, `// no activity yet` — match Geist Mono / italic / dim register.

## Round 2 — Trust + conversation

### Why this will be good
This stage takes the dashboard from 'shared notepad' to 'shared workspace with rules'. Captains and First Mates get destructive authority enforced both in UI and on the server (so a curl call cannot bypass it). Comment threads stop *fighting* the user — typing no longer gets interrupted by jumpy auto-merges, and @mentions actually notify people. Comment-level reactions add the same tactile signal layer to comments that we already have on stages. Production-ready means: 403s come back with a clean reason, the @ autocomplete works on keyboard alone, the 'N new' pill is unmistakable, and reactions persist across reload. Benchmark: Linear's role enforcement, Slack's @ autocomplete, Discord's reaction strip.

### The 4 deliverables

#### 1. Permissions: Captain/firstMate-only on destructive ops (server + UI)

**Intent:** Destructive ops on shared structure (archiving stages/pipelines/subtasks, deleting customPipelines, modifying workspace membership) require captain or firstMate role on the workspace that owns the affected pipeline. Crew users keep their open powers (claim, comment, react, assign self, drag own stages). Enforced both server-side (security) and client-side (UX).

**Files to touch:**
- `src/app/api/pipeline-state/route.ts` (PATCH handler) — for each PATCH body, identify which top-level keys are 'destructive structural':
  - `archivedStages`, `archivedPipelines`, `archivedSubtasks` (any change)
  - `customPipelines` (deletions only — additions stay open; detect by comparing keys present in old vs new)
  - `workspaces` (member additions/removals — any change to `captains`, `firstMates`, or `members` arrays)
  For each destructive key in the patch, derive the affected `workspaceId`:
    - For pipeline-scoped keys (archivedStages/archivedPipelines/archivedSubtasks/customPipelines): look up the pipeline → its workspaceId.
    - For `workspaces`: the patch body itself names the workspace.
  Then load the workspace from MongoDB and check `session.user.fixedUserId` is in `workspace.captains` OR `workspace.firstMates`. If not, return `403 { error: "FORBIDDEN", reason: "captain or first-mate role required" }`.
  If a single PATCH touches multiple workspaces, the check runs per workspace and ANY failure 403s the whole request (atomic — no partial writes).
- New: `src/lib/contexts/ModelContext.tsx` add `useRole(workspaceId: string): 'captain' | 'firstMate' | 'crew' | null`. Reads workspace from ModelContext state, returns role based on current user's `fixedUserId` membership in `captains` / `firstMates` / `members`. `null` means user is not in this workspace at all. Memoize on workspaceId + workspace state to avoid recompute storm.
- `src/components/Stage.tsx`, `src/components/Dashboard.tsx` (pipeline-list), `src/components/KanbanView.tsx` — gate pencil rendering: pencil renders only if `useRole(pipeline.workspaceId)` returns `'captain'` or `'firstMate'`. Replace the Round 1 placeholder `canEditPipeline()` with this hook.
- New: 'are you sure?' modal for destructive PIPELINE ops (archive pipeline, delete pipeline). Component: `src/components/ConfirmModal.tsx` — props `{ title, message, confirmLabel, danger: boolean, onConfirm, onCancel }`. Visual: dimmed backdrop, centered card with `t.bgCard`, title, message body listing consequences (e.g. 'this pipeline has 14 stages, 3 unclaimed' — pipeline ops resolve consequence text from current state), red `confirmLabel` button (`t.red`), `t.textMuted` cancel. Keyboard: Escape cancels, Enter confirms.
- Wire confirm modal into `archivePipeline` and `deletePipeline` handlers — they no longer fire immediately but open the modal first.

**Critical constraints:**
- Server check is the source of truth. UI gating is convenience; never assume client-side gating is enough.
- Crew permissions UNCHANGED — claim, comment, react, assign self, drag own stages, edit own subtasks. Do NOT regress these.
- 403 response shape MUST be `{ error: "FORBIDDEN", reason: "..." }`. Client surfaces this as a toast: 'this requires captain or first-mate role' — handle in `apiSync.ts` patchState wrapper, do not rely on each call site.
- The role check must run BEFORE the patch is applied — the request is rejected, no partial mutation.
- Performance: workspace lookup adds 1 read per PATCH. Acceptable. If it becomes a hot path, cache workspaces in route handler module scope (5s TTL) — not required for Round 2.
- Unauth users (no session) already get 401 from middleware; this layer adds 403 for authenticated-but-unprivileged.

**Acceptance criteria (QA):**
- Crew user → no pencil on pipeline header. Pencil on individual Stage card present (Stage 1 behavior — claim/comment editing not gated here).
- Captain user → pencil visible everywhere they have rights.
- curl `PATCH /api/pipeline-state` with crew session and `archivedPipelines` change → 403 with the named reason.
- Captain archives a pipeline with 14 stages → confirm modal lists '14 stages, N unclaimed' before archive fires.
- Modal Escape/cancel → no archive happens; confirm → archive fires + undo toast (Round 1 plumbing).

#### 2. Anti-jump comment polling

**Intent:** Stop comment-list jumps from interrupting users mid-sentence. When the user is typing in a comment input on stage X, polled comments for X are buffered, not merged. A 'N new' pill appears at the bottom of the popover; clicking it merges + scrolls to the newest.

**Files to touch:**
- `src/lib/contexts/ModelContext.tsx` — add `pendingNewComments: Record<stageId, CommentItem[]>`. Add helper `flushPendingComments(stageId)` which merges pending into `comments[stageId]` and clears the pending slice.
- The polling tick (wherever fetchState's comment delta merges into ModelContext) must check: for each new comment for stage X, is `commentOpen === X` AND `commentInput[X]?.trim().length > 0`? If yes → push to `pendingNewComments[X]`. Otherwise → merge directly into `comments[X]` (current behavior).
- `src/components/CommentPopover.tsx` (or wherever the comment popover lives — DEV identifies in Stage.tsx if not extracted) — render a sticky pill at the bottom: `↓ N new` only when `pendingNewComments[stageId]?.length > 0`. Click → call `flushPendingComments(stageId)` AND scroll the comment list to bottom. Visual: `t.accent` background pill, white text, hover lift.
- When the user closes the popover, auto-flush any remaining pending into the main list (so they don't reappear on next open).
- When the user clears the input (back to empty) while popover is still open → auto-flush pending.

**Critical constraints:**
- 'Typing' = `commentInput[stageId]` is non-empty AND `commentOpen === stageId`. Both conditions required.
- Pending comments must not be lost on dashboard unmount. Either persist to localStorage (preferred, key `binayah_pendingComments_<userId>`) OR flush on unmount. DEV picks; flush-on-unmount is simpler and acceptable for Round 2.
- Pill renders ABOVE the input, not as a toast — it must feel like part of the popover.
- N must update in real time as more polled comments stream in while the user is still typing.

**Acceptance criteria (QA):**
- Open comments on stage X, start typing in input. Have a teammate post a comment. List does NOT jump; pill '↓ 1 new' appears.
- Type more, teammate posts again — pill becomes '↓ 2 new'.
- Click pill → 2 new comments appear, list scrolls to bottom, pill disappears.
- Clear the input completely (delete what you typed) while popover open → 2 new comments auto-merge, no pill.
- Close popover with pending pending — reopening shows them all merged.

#### 3. @mentions in stage comments

**Intent:** Typing `@anna` in a stage comment does two things: (a) when posted, notifies Anna via the existing notification path, (b) while typing, opens an autocomplete dropdown of matching users.

**Files to touch:**
- `src/app/api/pipeline-state/comments/route.ts` (POST) — after the comment is saved successfully:
  - Import the SAME `parseMentions` and `notifyMentions` (or equivalent) from `src/app/api/pipeline-state/messages/route.ts`. If they are inline in messages/route.ts, extract them to `src/lib/mentions.ts` first as a small refactor.
  - Parse `text` for `@<word>`, look up users by `name` (case-insensitive, first-name match — same logic chat already uses).
  - For each mentioned user, call the existing notification fan-out (whatever messages/route.ts uses). Use `void notifyMentions(...)` — fire-and-forget pattern, do NOT await.
  - Skip self-mentions (don't notify the comment author about their own @mention).
- `src/components/CommentPopover.tsx` — autocomplete dropdown:
  - Detect `@` immediately preceded by space or string start, followed by letters being typed (regex: `/(^|\s)@(\w*)$/`).
  - When matched, render a popup positioned below the caret (or above if near viewport bottom — use `getBoundingClientRect`). Show up to 6 matching users by name prefix (case-insensitive). Each row: avatar + name. Active row highlighted with `t.accent + '22'`.

- Self-mention by Anna → no notification to Anna.
- Type `@xyz` (no match) → dropdown shows empty state or auto-closes (DEV picks; either is acceptable).

#### 4. Comment-level reactions

**Intent:** Each comment in a stage popover can be reacted to with the same six-emoji set used on stages. Toggling is atomic via a dedicated endpoint. Reaction strip renders below each comment, smaller than stage reactions.

- `src/lib/validate.ts` — add `commentReactions` to the PATCH key whitelist.

## Round 3 — Reach + momentum

### Why this will be good
This stage adds the texture that turns a working tool into a habit. Subtasks become *fluid* — drag them between parents without losing their reactions, comments, or assignments. Streaks reward consistency in a way that points alone cannot. The 'while you were away' digest converts re-opens into satisfying re-engagement instead of cold restarts. And the mobile 'today' view finally makes the dashboard usable on a phone for crew checking in between meetings. Production-ready means: subtask migrations are atomic and reversible, streaks compute correctly across DST and timezone edges, the digest never falsely fires after a quick refresh, and mobile swipe gestures don't fight scroll. Benchmark: Linear's mobile inbox, Strava's streak, Slack's 'unread since' digest.

### The 4 deliverables

#### 1. Drag subtask to a different parent stage

**Intent:** A subtask card can be dragged onto another *stage card* (not a kanban column) to migrate the subtask to that stage. All sibling state (reactions, comments, assignments, subtaskStages) follows the migration. Visual feedback distinguishes 'drop on stage card' from 'drop in column'.

**FILE NAMES — verified against actual codebase:**

- The subtask drag target is stage cards rendered inside `TasksView.tsx` and `Stage.tsx`.

**Files to touch:**
- `src/lib/contexts/ModelContext.tsx` — new handler `migrateSubtask(oldKey: SubtaskKey, newParentStageId: string)`:
  - Use `SubtaskKey.parse(oldKey)` to get `{ parentStageId: oldParent, subtaskId }`.
  - Build `newKey = SubtaskKey.make(newParentStageId, subtaskId)`.
  - Atomically in one state update: copy `subtasks[oldKey] → subtasks[newKey]`, transfer `reactions[oldKey] → reactions[newKey]`, `subtaskStages[oldKey] → subtaskStages[newKey]`. Then DELETE all old-key entries.
  - **`assignments` note:** `assignments` is localStorage-only — it is NOT in the PATCH whitelist and cannot be PATCHed to the server. Handle assignment transfer by updating local `assignments` state (`setAssignments`) alongside the other state updates. If you want assignments persisted to MongoDB, first ADD "assignments" to `PATCH_KEY_WHITELIST` in `src/lib/validate.ts` AND to the PipelineState Mongoose schema — otherwise skip server persistence for assignments and accept localStorage-only.
  - **`commentReactions` note:** Comments belong to stages, not subtasks. `commentReactions` is keyed by `${stageId}::${commentId}` where stageId = stage name string. Subtasks have NO per-subtask comment threads. When a subtask migrates, there are NO commentReaction keys to transfer. Do NOT attempt to remap commentReaction keys — there is nothing to remap.
  - Single PATCH to server with all the new state in one body: `{ subtasks: newSubtasks, reactions: newReactions, subtaskStages: newSubtaskStages }`. All three keys are in `PATCH_KEY_WHITELIST`. Do NOT do this as multiple sequential PATCHes — partial migration is the bug class to avoid.
- `src/components/TasksView.tsx` (stage cards inside) and `src/components/Stage.tsx` — accept drop events from subtask cards. While a subtask is being dragged:
  - Stage cards get a dashed `t.accent` border on dragOver.
  - Kanban columns become DIMMER (`opacity: 0.6`) so the user understands columns aren't the target.
  - Drop on a stage card → call `migrateSubtask(draggedKey, dropTargetStageId)` if `dropTargetStageId !== oldParent`. Same-parent drops are no-ops.

- Drag preview: subtask card with semi-transparent background; mouse cursor 'grabbing'.

**Critical constraints:**
- ALWAYS use `SubtaskKey.make / SubtaskKey.parse` — never raw `::` splits.
- Migration must be atomic in the SINGLE PATCH body. If the server rejects, the client state has not been mutated yet (optimistic UI is OK only if rollback is implemented and tested — DEV decides; pessimistic is acceptable for Round 3).
- The undo stack from Round 1 SHOULD also catch this: pushUndo `{ label: 'moved subtask <title> to <newParent>', inverse: () => migrateSubtask(newKey, oldParent) }`. Migrate-back is symmetric.

**Acceptance criteria (QA):**
- Drag subtask from stage A onto stage card B → subtask migrates, reactions follow, subtaskStages follow.
- Stage card B shows dashed accent border during drag-over.
- Kanban columns are visibly dimmed during subtask drag.
- Drop subtask back on its current parent → no-op (no PATCH, no toast).
- Cmd+Z (Round 1 undo) → subtask returns to A, all state intact.

#### 2. Streaks (server-derived, client-rendered)

**Intent:** A user's streak is the count of consecutive UTC days (working back from today) where they appear as actor on a claim, comment, or status_change activity entry. Pure derived state — server emits, client renders.

- `src/lib/contexts/ModelContext.tsx` — store `streakByUser` from GET. No client write path.

**Acceptance criteria (QA):**
- User with activity today + yesterday + 2 days ago → streak = 3 → renders '🔥3'.
- User with no activity today → streak = 0 → no flame rendered.
- User with activity today only → streak = 1 → no flame rendered (>=2 gate).
- User active for 7 consecutive days → '🔥7'.
- Reload page → streak unchanged (server-derived).
- curl PATCH attempting to set streakByUser → 400 (not in whitelist).

#### 3. "While you were away" digest modal

**Intent:** When a user opens the Dashboard after >12h absence (or first visit), show a centered modal recapping what they missed: claims by teammates, mentions of them, status changes on their claimed stages.

**Files to touch:**
- localStorage key: `binayah_lastSession_<fixedUserId>` storing ms timestamp. Helpers in `src/lib/storage.ts` (additive, no SCHEMA_VERSION bump).
- `src/components/Dashboard.tsx` (or new `WhileAwayDigest.tsx` component imported there) — on mount:
  - Read `lastSession`. If absent OR `Date.now() - lastSession > 12 * 60 * 60 * 1000`, show modal.
  - Compute three sections from activityLog:
    - (a) `stages claimed by teammates since lastSession` — `entries where type === 'claim' AND user !== currentUser AND time > lastSession`. Top 5 newest.
    - (b) `comments mentioning you since lastSession` — entries of type `comment` where `text` contains `@<currentUser.name>` (case-insensitive first-name match). Top 5 newest.
    - (c) `status changes on stages you claimed since lastSession` — entries of type `status_change` where the stage is in `claims[stageKey]?.includes(currentUser.id)` AND `time > lastSession`. Top 5 newest.
  - Each entry row: `<AvatarC user={actor} />` + one-line description + relative time (`time-ago`).
  - Section header copy in product voice: `// claims since you left`, `// you were mentioned`, `// status changes on your stages`.
  - Single 'got it' button styled with `t.accent` background. Click → set `lastSession = Date.now()`, close modal.
  - On Dashboard unmount: ALWAYS update `lastSession = Date.now()` (so a quick refresh doesn't trigger digest on next visit).
- Modal visual: backdrop `rgba(0,0,0,0.6)`, centered card on `t.bgCard`, max-width 480px, padding generous, three section blocks with thin `t.border` dividers.
- If ALL three sections are empty (user genuinely missed nothing) → show modal with copy `// quiet while you were gone`. Single got-it button.

**Critical constraints:**
- 12h threshold is a literal — exactly 12 hours.
- `lastSession` is per-user via fixedUserId — multiple users on shared devices isolated.
- Modal must NOT block the dashboard render — render dashboard underneath, then mount modal as a portal/overlay.
- Escape key dismisses (same as 'got it' — also writes lastSession).
- Mention detection in section (b): reuse the SAME logic as Stage 2 mention parser. Import the helper from `src/lib/mentions.ts` (created in Stage 2 — confirmed to exist). DO NOT fork.
- Keep computation client-side — activityLog is already in ModelContext. No new API.
- If `activityLog.length === 0` → skip modal (first-ever load with no data is not a digest event).

**Acceptance criteria (QA):**
- First-ever visit (no lastSession) → modal appears with whatever content the activityLog has. After dismiss, refresh → no modal.
- Refresh dashboard within 1 minute → no modal (lastSession was just written).
- Modify lastSession to 13h ago via devtools → reload → modal appears.
- All three sections empty → 'quiet while you were gone' single-line message; got it dismisses.
- Mention section: ensure it picks up @currentUser comments only, case-insensitive.
- Escape key → modal dismisses, lastSession updated.

#### 4. Mobile single-column 'today' view

**Intent:** At ≤640px viewport, the kanban is REPLACED entirely with a single-column vertical list filtered to the current user's claimed/assigned stages, sorted by status priority. Each row has one primary action and supports swipe gestures.

**Critical constraints:**
- UTC day buckets — never local time. `entry.time` is a ms timestamp; convert via `new Date(t).toISOString().slice(0, 10)`.
- Three activity types only: `claim`, `comment`, `status_change`. Reactions and other types do NOT contribute.
- No client write path — never let the client patch streakByUser. `streakByUser` must NOT be added to `PATCH_KEY_WHITELIST` in validate.ts.
- Performance: compute O(n) over activityLog with a hashmap of user → set of day-strings.
- Visual: flame emoji 🔥 + integer, same line as points, separated by single space. No special animation.

**Acceptance criteria (QA):**
- User with activity today + yesterday + 2 days ago → streak = 3 → renders '🔥3'.
- User with no activity today → streak = 0 → no flame rendered.
- User with activity today only → streak = 1 → no flame rendered (>=2 gate).
- User active for 7 consecutive days → '🔥7'.
- Reload page → streak unchanged (server-derived).
- curl PATCH attempting to set streakByUser → 400 (not in whitelist).

#### 3. "While you were away" digest modal

**Intent:** When a user opens the Dashboard after >12h absence (or first visit), show a centered modal recapping what they missed: claims by teammates, mentions of them, status changes on their claimed stages.

**Files to touch:**
- localStorage key: `binayah_lastSession_<fixedUserId>` storing ms timestamp. Helpers in `src/lib/storage.ts` (additive, no SCHEMA_VERSION bump).
- `src/components/Dashboard.tsx` (or new `WhileAwayDigest.tsx` component imported there) — on mount:
  - Read `lastSession`. If absent OR `Date.now() - lastSession > 12 * 60 * 60 * 1000`, show modal.
  - Compute three sections from activityLog:
    - (a) `stages claimed by teammates since lastSession` — `entries where type === 'claim' AND user !== currentUser AND time > lastSession`. Top 5 newest.
    - (b) `comments mentioning you since lastSession` — entries of type `comment` where `text` contains `@<currentUser.name>` (case-insensitive first-name match). Top 5 newest.
    - (c) `status changes on stages you claimed since lastSession` — entries of type `status_change` where the stage is in `claims[stageKey]?.includes(currentUser.id)` AND `time > lastSession`. Top 5 newest.
  - Each entry row: `<AvatarC user={actor} />` + one-line description + relative time (`time-ago`).
  - Section header copy in product voice: `// claims since you left`, `// you were mentioned`, `// status changes on your stages`.
  - Single 'got it' button styled with `t.accent` background. Click → set `lastSession = Date.now()`, close modal.
  - On Dashboard unmount: ALWAYS update `lastSession = Date.now()` (so a quick refresh doesn't trigger digest on next visit).
- Modal visual: backdrop `rgba(0,0,0,0.6)`, centered card on `t.bgCard`, max-width 480px, padding generous, three section blocks with thin `t.border` dividers.
- If ALL three sections are empty (user genuinely missed nothing) → show modal with copy `// quiet while you were gone`. Single got-it button.

**Critical constraints:**
- 12h threshold is a literal — exactly 12 hours.
- `lastSession` is per-user via fixedUserId — multiple users on shared devices isolated.
- Modal must NOT block the dashboard render — render dashboard underneath, then mount modal as a portal/overlay.
- Escape key dismisses (same as 'got it' — also writes lastSession).
- Mention detection in section (b): reuse the SAME logic as Stage 2 mention parser. Import the helper from `src/lib/mentions.ts` (created in Stage 2 — confirmed to exist). DO NOT fork.
- Keep computation client-side — activityLog is already in ModelContext. No new API.
- If `activityLog.length === 0` → skip modal (first-ever load with no data is not a digest event).

**Acceptance criteria (QA):**
- First-ever visit (no lastSession) → modal appears with whatever content the activityLog has. After dismiss, refresh → no modal.
- Refresh dashboard within 1 minute → no modal (lastSession was just written).
- Modify lastSession to 13h ago via devtools → reload → modal appears.
- All three sections empty → 'quiet while you were gone' single-line message; got it dismisses.
- Mention section: ensure it picks up @currentUser comments only, case-insensitive.
- Escape key → modal dismisses, lastSession updated.

#### 4. Mobile single-column 'today' view

**Intent:** At ≤640px viewport, the kanban is REPLACED entirely with a single-column vertical list filtered to the current user's claimed/assigned stages, sorted by status priority. Each row has one primary action and supports swipe gestures.

**FILE NAMES — verified against actual codebase:**
- The kanban component is `src/components/TasksView.tsx` (NOT KanbanView.tsx).
- `useIsMobile` in `src/hooks/useIsMobile.ts` already accepts a `breakpoint` parameter — call `useIsMobile(640)` directly. No new hook needed.

**Files to touch:**
- `src/components/TasksView.tsx` — at viewport ≤640px (use `useIsMobile(640)`), do NOT render the multi-column kanban. Instead render `<TodayView />`.
- New: `src/components/TodayView.tsx` — vertical list:
  - Filter source: all stages where `claims[stageKey]?.includes(currentUser.id)` OR `assignments[stageKey] === currentUser.id`.
  - Sort: status priority — `in-progress > planned > active > concept` (using the canonical 4 statuses in this codebase; 'blocked'/'done' do not exist).
  - Each row: compact card (~64px height), title, status pill (color per statusColor()), claim/done state visual, ONE primary action button:
    - Status `concept` or `planned` (unclaimed by me) → `+ claim` button.
    - Status `in-progress` AND mine → `mark done` button (= bump status to `active`).
    - Other → `view` button (expands the stage in a bottom sheet).
  - Use `t.bgCard`, `t.border`, theme tokens throughout.
- Swipe gestures (raw Pointer Events API preferred — no new library dep; or `react-swipeable` if it makes the code cleaner — ~3KB, acceptable):
  - Swipe-right on a row → toggle claim/unclaim for current user.
  - Swipe-left on a row → open comment popover (existing component, in a BottomSheet on mobile per project pattern).
  - Swipe must require >40px horizontal travel to avoid mis-fire from vertical scroll.
  - Cancel swipe on first vertical movement >10px (the user is scrolling, not swiping).
- The kanban is dropped ENTIRELY at ≤640px — no horizontal scroll columns, no pinch zoom. It's a list.
- Empty state: `// nothing on your plate yet — claim something` in product voice.

**Critical constraints:**
- The 640px breakpoint is BELOW the existing 768px (which switches sidebar off). So between 640 and 768, the user sees the existing kanban with no left sidebar. Below 640, this new TodayView. Clean breakpoint — don't break the existing desktop kanban.
- Swipe-right toggle is OPTIMISTIC — show the new state immediately, server reconciles.
- Status pill, claim button, primary action — all must use theme tokens. Mobile is not a second-class citizen for theming.
- Do not introduce hammer.js (heavyweight, abandoned-ish).

**Acceptance criteria (QA):**
- iPhone viewport (390×844): see vertical list, no kanban columns.
- Tablet viewport (768×1024): see existing kanban (unchanged).
- List shows only stages I have claimed or am assigned to.
- Sort: in-progress at top, then planned, then active, then concept.
- Swipe-right on an unclaimed stage → claim toggles on.
- Swipe-left on any stage → comment popover opens (BottomSheet on mobile).
- Vertical scroll works normally — swipes don't capture vertical motion.
- Empty state copy in product voice.
- TypeScript clean.

### Stage 3 dependencies
- Builds on Stage 1: subtask migrate uses pushUndo (Round 1 stack). The activity tab from Round 1 will reflect migrate operations naturally if the migrate handler emits an activity event (DEV decides; recommend yes — type 'subtask_migrated', target = newParentStageId).
- Builds on Stage 2: 'while you were away' digest mention parsing reuses `src/lib/mentions.ts` (confirmed to exist). Server permission check applies if any captain-only ops fire from mobile view (in practice: claim/done are crew-allowed, so no friction).

### Files DEV will touch (summary)
- `src/lib/contexts/ModelContext.tsx` (migrateSubtask handler, streakByUser slice from GET)
- `src/components/TasksView.tsx` (subtask drop on stage cards; mobile breakpoint switch) — NOTE: this is the actual kanban file, not KanbanView.tsx
- `src/components/Stage.tsx` (drop target rendering)
- `src/components/TodayView.tsx` (NEW)
- `src/components/WhileAwayDigest.tsx` (NEW)
- `src/components/Dashboard.tsx` (mount digest, lastSession lifecycle, render streak in team bar)
- `src/app/api/pipeline-state/route.ts` (GET emits streakByUser)
- `src/lib/storage.ts` (lastSession helpers — additive)
- `src/hooks/useIsMobile.ts` (no changes needed — hook already accepts breakpoint param; just call useIsMobile(640))
- `src/lib/validate.ts` (optionally add "assignments" to PATCH_KEY_WHITELIST if assignment persistence is desired)
- (no schema changes — staying on v3 per task scope)

### Apply DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md
- 'Mobile is the default' — TodayView is not a watered-down version of the kanban; it's the *right* view for the surface.
- 'Loading is activity, not waiting' — digest modal renders instantly on mount; do not block on a fetch.
- 'Empty states are part of the game' — '// quiet while you were gone', '// nothing on your plate yet — claim something'.
- 'Gamification must feel earned' — streak only renders at >=2. A 1-day streak is not a streak.
- 'Motion has meaning' — swipe gestures use existing 150ms timing; no new motion vocabulary.
